precedents.case: State of Rajasthan v. Hemraj Vardar
precedents.holding: Sampling without independent witnesses in corruption cases renders the FSL report unreliable.

precedents.case: H.N. Rishbud v. State of Delhi
precedents.holding: Defective investigation does not necessarily vitiate the trial unless it results in miscarriage of justice.
Economic offences constitute a class apart and need to be visited with a different approach in bail.
Departmental enquiry findings can be used to challenge criminal charge-sheets if the allegations are identical.

precedents.case: State of Maharashtra v. Som Nath Thapa
precedents.holding: At the stage of framing charges, the court has to see if there is a 'prima facie' case.
Bail is the rule, jail is the exception - reiteration in the context of PMLA.

precedents.case: Shafhi Mohammad v. State of Himachal Pradesh
precedents.holding: Admissibility of electronic evidence without Section 65B certificate.

precedents.case: State of Haryana v. Bhajan Lal
precedents.holding: Guidelines for quashing FIR under Section 482 CrPC.

statutes.title: Criminal Breach of Trust by Public Servant
Criminal Misconduct by a Public Servant
Offence relating to public servant being bribed
